channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Georgia House approves the second part of sweeping plan to limit lawsuits
item.description: The second part of Georgia Republican Gov. Brian Kemp’s top priority to limit lawsuits and large verdicts won final approval from the House on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Charlotte Kramon, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US Homeland Security Secretary Noem visits Colombia to discuss immigration and crime
item.description: BOGOTÁ (AP) — U.S. Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem landed in Colombia on Thursday as part of a tour of three Latin American nations to discuss immigration, crime and deportation.
item.dc:creator: ASTRID SUÁREZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Conservative group warns of high costs as US influence fades in Latin America
item.description: Paraguay, Argentina and Panama are three key nations in the southern hemisphere the new administration would be wise to pay attention to, experts say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New poll shows where Trump stands among Americans 9 weeks into his 2nd presidency
item.description: President Donald Trump's approval ratings are slightly underwater in the latest polls, nine weeks into his second tour of duty in the White House.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: EXCLUSIVE: GOP moves to defund ‘chronically biased’ NPR, PBS after disastrous hearing
item.description: After a hearing by the DOGE Subcommittee, Rep. Ronny Jackson, R-Texas, is introducing a bill to pull government funding from NPR and PBS, which he says have been “pushing Democrat talking points.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Walz announces remote work changes for Minnesota state agency employees
item.description: State agency employees will have to work in-person for at least 50 percent of scheduled workdays starting June 1. The policy change provides an exemption for employees who live more than 75 miles away from their primary work location.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Turkish student at Tufts University detained, video shows masked people handcuffing her
item.description: A lawyer for a Turkish national and doctoral student at Tufts University says she has been detained by Department of Homeland Security agents without explanation.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump says he 'always thought' Waltz was responsible for Signal chat scandal
item.description: President Donald Trump told reporters on Wednesday that he "always thought" national security advisor Mike Waltz was responsible for the Signal chat scandal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: In Jamaica, Marco Rubio vows to revisit US travel warning
item.description: KINGSTON, Jamaica (AP) — U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Wednesday the State Department will re-evaluate its travel warnings for Jamaica and other countries to ensure they reflect real conditions on the ground.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Homeland Security Secretary Noem visits the El Salvador prison where deported Venezuelans are held
item.description: TECOLUCA, El Salvador (AP) — U.S. Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem on Wednesday visited the high-security El Salvador prison where Venezuelans who the Trump administration alleges are gang members have been held since their removal from the United States.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA and ALEX BRANDON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Appeals court won't lift order that barred Trump administration from deportations under wartime law
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal appeals court refused Wednesday to lift an order barring the Trump administration from deporting Venezuelan migrants to El Salvador under an 18th century wartime law. A split three-judge panel of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Is the fight over? Florida bill to allow guns on campus gets shot down
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (WFLA) — A rare rejection happened at the state capitol as a Republican-led bill was voted down by a fellow GOP lawmaker. Senate Bill 814 would have allowed concealed carry on college campuses, but it died in its first committee stop.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The Signal attack plan messages: What we do (and don't) know
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The revelation that President Donald Trump's most senior national security officials posted the specifics of a military attack to a chat group that included a journalist hours before the attack took place in Yemen has raised many questions.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 5 takeaways from House hearing on NPR, PBS
item.description: The heads of the nation's top public broadcasters faced an intense grilling from lawmakers Wednesday over their outlets’ funding and editorial objectivity.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5

item.title: Judge says Justice Department attacked her character to 'impugn the integrity' of US judicial system
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge accused the Justice Department on Wednesday of attacking her character in an effort to undermine the integrity of the judicial system, forcefully pushing back against the Trump administration's criticism of the courts for rulings that blocked parts of the president'
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 5 takeaways from House hearing on NPR, PBS
item.description: The heads of the nation's top public broadcasters faced an intense grilling from lawmakers Wednesday on Capitol Hill over their outlets’ funding and editorial objectivity.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: Hakeem Jeffries calls for Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth to resign
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries tells NewsNation that Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth should lose his job.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota Republicans look to DOGE model for state government efficiency commission
item.description: The commission would consist of a panel of six citizens who would investigate fraud in state agencies and potential conflicts of interest in the Legislature. Three members would be appointed by Republicans and three by Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: JD Vance joining his wife for Greenland trip on Friday
item.description: The delegation is set to tour the northernmost security installation for the U.S. military, the Space Force's Pituffik Space Base.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Intelligence leaders: We didn’t share classified information in Signal chat group
item.description: Some of the nation’s top intelligence officials will appear before Congress in a pair of hearings this week. Two were participants in a widely criticized war plans group chat on Signal.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman, MPR News

item.title: Oz moves closer to confirmation as Medicare, Medicaid chief
item.description: Mehmet Oz is a step closer to being confirmed as the next head of the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services, after the Senate Finance Committee on Tuesday advanced his nomination. The vote was 14-13 along party lines.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: Judge won't lift block on Trump use of Alien Enemies Act to deport Venezuelans
item.description: U.S. District Court Judge James Boasberg declined to lift a restraining order barring the Trump administration from using the Alien Enemies Act to deport Venezuelans, determining migrants are likely to succeed in pushing for hearings on whether they are in a gang.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5
